title: artemisia campestris var. douglasiana
intro: Artemisia campestris var. douglasiana, also known as Douglas' sagebrush, is a variety of the sagebrush plant that is native to the western United States and Canada. It has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, making it useful for treating a range of health issues. The health benefits of Douglas' sagebrush include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate respiratory issues, and soothe digestive problems, as well as its potential to reduce the severity of colds and flu. Herbal preparations of Douglas' sagebrush include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall health and well-being.
